title: Fragaria abortiva
taxonomy: Fragaria abortiva is a member of the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family includes many species of trees, shrubs, and herbs, and is one of the largest and most widespread plant families in the world. Fragaria abortiva is a genus within the Rosaceae family that is primarily composed of wild strawberries and their close relatives. The genus Fragaria is native to the Northern Hemisphere and is widely distributed across temperate and subarctic regions. Fragaria abortiva, like other species in the Fragaria genus, is a type of herbaceous perennial plant.
uses: Fragaria abortiva, also known as wild strawberry, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal remedies, often in combination with other plants, to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Some studies suggest that Fragaria abortiva may have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and infection prevention. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

preparations: Fragaria abortiva is sometimes used to make herbal preparations, but its effectiveness and safety for human use are not well-documented. side_effects: Fragaria abortiva, commonly known as wild strawberry, may cause side effects due to its potential allergenic properties. Ingesting the plant can lead to gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. It is also believed to interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. The plant's allergenic properties may cause skin irritation, hives, and itching in some individuals. If you are considering using Fragaria abortiva, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional to discuss potential risks and benefits.
